hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The role must be set to the literal string 'system', and the parts array must be exactly as provided in the input ('from_input_parts_array'), resulting in the structure {parts:input_parts, role:system}.

Set the cachedContent field to the literal string 'my_cached_context', systemInstruction to 'default_system_prompt_v2', tools to an empty array [], and the model to the literal string 'models/gemini-1.5-flash-latest'.
The client header value is 'client/version nodejs/18.16.0' (substituting PACKAGE with the actual node runtime version) and __PACKAGE_VERSION__ resolves to the literal string '0.3.4'.

context_pointers.actual_meaning: The text 'standard_url=https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/{model}:task?alt=sse' and 'generateContentRequest.model=modelDefaultValue' refer to a static endpoint path and a default model string that should be hardcoded in the routing config.

context_pointers.vague_reference: the logic used in the text formatter unit tests
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The phrase 'concatenated_text' refers to a specific concatenation algorithm that handles multi-linguality and special characters, which is detailed in the test suite under 'responseRenderingUnitTests.js'.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_cache_resource_requests.json.description: Cached content operations normalize resource names, construct create/list/get/update/delete requests with pagination and expiration fields, and reject missing or conflicting expiration inputs.

feature1_content_request_formatting.json.description: Text prompts and part arrays are converted into a canonical single user content request while preserving extra request configuration.

feature2_system_instruction_formatting.j.description: A caller-supplied system instruction is normalized into a system role content object, whether supplied as plain text, a text part, or role-less content.

feature3_token_count_request_formatting..description: Token-count inputs are wrapped as a generation-style request and may inherit model-level context such as system instruction, tools, and cached content references.

feature4_model_request_construction.json.description: Model RPC requests produce deterministic URLs, request headers, methods, streaming query markers, custom client labels, custom headers, timeout signals, and normalized backend errors.

cases.expected_output:
url=https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/model-name:generateContent?alt=sse
method=POST
content_type=application/json
api_key_header=key
client_header=client/version genai-js/__PACKAGE_VERSION__
customer_header=customerHeaderValue
has_abort_signal=true

error=http_error
message=extra info
status=500
status_text=Server Error

feature5_response_rendering.json.description: A completed response exposes concatenated text, ordered tool-call payloads, mixed text/tool-call content, and executable-code output as developer-readable text.

cases.expected_output:
text=Some text and some more text
function_calls=[]

text=some text and more text
function_calls=[{"args":{"location":"Mountain View, CA","movie":"Barbie","time":"20:00"},"name":"find_times"}]

parts.text: here's how to print hello world

cases.expected_output:
text=here's how to print hello world
```PYTHON
print("hello world")
```

```
hello world
```

function_calls=[]

feature6_blocked_response_signals.json.description: A response blocked by safety feedback or unsafe finish reasons reports the block scope and reason instead of returning normal generated content.

feature7_chat_history_validation.json.description: Conversation history is accepted only when it begins with user content and every turn contains non-empty parts compatible with that role.

feature8_model_response_validity.json.description: A model reply is valid for conversation state only when it contains at least one meaningful text, tool-call, or tool-response part.

feature9_file_resource_requests.json.description: File resource operations normalize file identifiers and upload metadata, construct the correct HTTP method and URL, include upload protocol headers for multipart uploads, and reject invalid identifiers or missing MIME types.

cases.expected_output:
task=upload
method=POST
url=https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/upload/v1beta/files
upload_protocol=multipart
body_has_mime=true
body_has_name=true
body_has_display_name=true
result={"file":{"uri":"https://yourfile.here/filename"}}

_constraints.input_condition: Input is a string
_constraints.output_rule: Convert string into single part with role user

_constraints.input_condition: Input is an array of strings
_constraints.output_rule: Convert array of strings into single part with role user

_constraints.input_condition: Input is an array containing mixed strings and objects with text keys
_constraints.output_rule: Flatten into single part with role user

_constraints.input_condition: Input object contains 'contents' array and 'generationConfig' object
_constraints.output_rule: Extract contents, map to new structure, preserve generationConfig

_constraints.input_condition: Input 'systemInstruction' is a string
_constraints.output_rule: Wrap in parts array with role system

_constraints.input_condition: Input 'systemInstruction' is object with 'text' key
_constraints.output_rule: Wrap text in parts array with role system

_constraints.input_condition: Input 'systemInstruction' is object with 'parts' array
_constraints.output_rule: Use provided parts with role system

_constraints.input_condition: Input object contains 'request' and 'modelDefaults' with 'model' only
Input object contains full 'modelDefaults' (model, systemInstruction, tools, cachedContent)
_constraints.output_rule: Wrap content in generateContentRequest, merge modelDefaults properties

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'url'
_constraints.output_rule: Construct URL based on model type and task

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'construct'
_constraints.output_rule: Construct URL for standard model, add streaming flag, add headers

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'construct' AND client_header provided
_constraints.output_rule: Format client header with version and package version

_constraints.input_condition: mode is 'construct' AND customHeader starts with 'x-goog-api-client'
mode is 'fetch_error'
Input candidate has content with text parts
Input candidate has content with functionCall parts
Input candidate has mixed text and functionCall parts
Input has promptFeedback with blockReason SAFETY
Input candidate has finishReason SAFETY
History starts with role 'model'
History turn has empty parts array
History is valid (starts with user, non-empty parts)
Response has no candidates
Candidate has content with non-empty text part
Candidate has content with functionCall part
Candidate has only empty text part
Operation is metadata and missing mimeType
Name starts with 'files/'
Operation is delete and name is empty
Operation is upload
